nameMeanings.name: deLucy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Yorkshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name for someone from Lassy in Calvados, France (see Lacy ), with the preposition de ‘from’.

variants.name: Delacey
meanings: English (East Yorkshire): of Norman origin, a variant of   Delacy  .

variants.name: Lacy
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lassy in Calvados, named from a Gaulish personal name Lascius (of uncertain origin and meaning) + the locative suffix -acum.

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: Emma deLucy was born in 1068, in Domfront, Orne, Lower Normandy, France. She married Henry Maunsel in 1089, in Lucé, Orne, Lower Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Emma deLucy(은)는 1068년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 돔프론트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1089년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 뤼ce에서 Henry Maunsel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 사망했다.
